question: What is the primary problem your project aims to solve?
answer: Automate manual workflow - Replace time-consuming manual processes with automated workflows

question: What specific manual workflow are you automating?
answer: Managing scattered instruction files across a monorepo - Keeping CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files consistent and up-to-date across multiple packages, AND Remembering and applying new rules - Capturing lessons learned during development and applying them consistently

question: What's the core value proposition of this tool?
answer: All of the above: Single source of truth - Centralize AI instruction rules and propagate them consistently across the entire monorepo; Developer productivity - Reduce time spent managing instruction files and remembering rules; Knowledge capture - Make it easy to capture and apply lessons learned during development

question: What is in scope for the initial version of this tool?
answer: Core CLI commands (init, build, add, list) for managing instruction files AND Full monorepo support - Complete support for global and package-level instruction files with imports

question: What should be explicitly OUT OF SCOPE for the initial version?
answer: All of the above: Advanced IDE integrations (VSCode extensions, editor plugins, etc.); AI framework-specific features beyond file generation; Cloud/hosted service (keep it local CLI only, no web service or cloud sync)

question: Who are your primary target users?
answer: Individual developers - Solo developers working on personal or small projects

question: What are the primary goals of individual developers using this tool?
answer: Maintain consistency - Keep AI instruction files consistent across their monorepo packages AND Capture knowledge - Quickly capture and apply lessons learned during development sessions

question: What are the main pain points developers experience with current AI instruction file management?
answer: All of the above: Manual synchronization - Having to manually copy/paste changes to multiple CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files; Forgetting to update - Remembering which files need updates when adding new rules; Loss of knowledge - Forgetting to document lessons learned after a development session

question: What is the primary use case for this tool?
answer: All of the above: Initial setup - Initialize a new monorepo with centralized instruction management; Adding new rules - Quickly add a new rule after discovering an AI issue during development; Propagating changes - Update all instruction files after modifying a global rule

question: How will you measure success for this tool?
answer: Time savings - Reduce time spent managing instruction files by X%

question: What specific metric or target would indicate successful time savings?
answer: Eliminate repetitive work - Zero time spent on manual copy/paste of rules

question: What are the primary technical constraints for this project?
answer: Must work across multiple AI frameworks (support both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md) AND File system based only (no database or external services). Note: Only targeting macOS initially

question: Are there any external dependencies this tool requires?
answer: Node.js runtime only - Just need Node.js/npm installed

question: What is the target timeline for the initial version?
answer: 1-2 weeks - Quick MVP to validate the concept

question: What are the must-have features for the MVP within the 1-2 week timeline?
answer: Full command set - All commands (init, build, add, list) with complete features

question: For the init command, what should be the default behavior when existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files are found?
answer: Create backup files of existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files (e.g., CLAUDE.md.backup) and then create fresh managed files

question: For the build command, how should the tool handle comment markers in the generated files?
answer: Use HTML-style comments - <!-- BEGIN AGENT-INSTRUCTION --> and <!-- END AGENT-INSTRUCTION -->

question: For the add command, what interface should it use to collect rule information?
answer: Command-line arguments - agent-instruction add "Rule content" --title "Title" --rule="rule-file"

question: Should the tool support a Claude Code skill that AI agents can use to manage instructions?
answer: Yes, include a skill - Create a skill so AI agents can use agent-instruction commands

question: What are the most important non-functional requirements for this tool?
answer: Ease of use - Simple, intuitive commands with clear output AND Performance - Fast execution even with large monorepos

question: What key assumptions are you making about this project?
answer: All of the above: Users understand monorepo concepts; Users want centralized management (prefer a single source of truth); JSON format is acceptable (users are comfortable editing JSON files for configuration)

question: What are the biggest risks to this project's success?
answer: None - No major risks identified
